response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with broad full-stack, systems, and testing experience, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, far short of 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience listed, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript investment-platform role, not capital markets. Low-latency DLang work shows relevant performance-engineering instincts, but the mandatory skill gaps are too large.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec) at Dunnhumby using async I/O and caching
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, plus DevOps and scripting breadth
Long, stable career with strong communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and courses
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ (2014–2015), none on the Windows platform recently
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: No capital-markets or fixed-income domain background
